Items.Title: 我的新 MacBook Air Beats M1 Max | My New MacBook Air Beats M1 Max
Items.Description:
这太令人震惊了……我刚换了笔记本电脑，我以为我是从 64gb（14.1 英寸版本）的“顶级”M1 Max 降级到“只有”24gb RAM 的“微型”MacBook Air M2。 结果我错了。 对于我作为 Java 开发人员的用例，新的 M2 似乎明显更快。 起初我很震惊，但回想起来我想这是有道理的。

我最近离开了我在 Lightrun 的工作。 我通常会购买自己的笔记本电脑，因为我不喜欢在工作或处理个人事务时不断切换设备。 但由于我在 Lightrun 工作了很长时间，所以我接受了他们提供的笔记本电脑。 拿到新笔记本电脑一年后，我发现自己离开了公司。 所以可以说这是一个大错误。 结果不是。

我想买同样的机器。 我对 M1 Max 非常满意。 它功能强大、速度快、重量轻，并且电池续航时间长。 它运行很酷，看起来很酷。 我向一家服务最差的当地供应商下了订单。 结果取消了。 然后我开始环顾四周。

我最初对 MacBook Air 不屑一顾。 我过去有几台 MacBook Air，它们在某些方面很有用。 但我现在做了很多视频编辑，我也受不了它们的锋利边缘。 在某些情况下，握住它们会不舒服。 新款 MacBook airs 终于增加了 RAM。 不像我想要的那样达到 32gb，但 24gb 已经比旧设备的微小 16gb 有了很大的改进。 它们也是黑色的，而且比同等的专业版便宜得多。 我非常灵活，既因为我旅行，也因为我到处工作。 对我来说，轻薄设备是一个巨大的优势。 您可以在苹果网站上查看比较工具。

它们确实有两个很大的缺点：

  • 没有 HDMI 端口 - 这有点糟糕。 在没有加密狗的情况下在会议上插入是很好的。 但这不是我经常做的事情，AirPlay 适用于大多数其他用例。
  • 只有一台外接显示器——我工作时不使用外接显示器，所以这对我来说不是问题。 如果您是需要两台显示器才能工作的类型，那么这款笔记本电脑不适合您。

因为两者对我来说都不是问题，而且其他好处也很大。 我存了一些钱买了空气。 我预计会受到性能影响。 事实证明，我的性能得到了重大提升！

移民

我使用 Time Machine 备份我的旧 Mac 并将其恢复到新 Mac。 在安装的软件和设置方面，两个设备应该是相同的。 在旧机器上运行的东西也应该在新机器上运行。 包括所有可能减慢速度的遗留问题。 但是，我不认为我的发现是科学的，因为这不是一个干净的环境。 一切都基于我的用例。 专业网站对常见用例有更好的基准。 我建议参考他们以获得更完整的图片。

但是，对我来说，机器要好得多。 可能最引人注目的是 IDE 启动。 我的大部分日常工作都使用 IntelliJ/IDEA Ultimate。 在完成我的上一本书（您现在可以预订）之后，我刚刚开始写一本新书，为此我安装了一个新版本的 IntelliJ Community Edition。 它不包括我典型安装中的所有插件和设置。 这是检查 IDE 启动时间的完美环境。 请注意，我使用秒表应用程序对此进行了测量，并不理想。 我通过单击图标启动秒表，并在 IDE 完全加载代号一项目时停止。

  • 工商管理硕士 M2 24gb - 4.54

这是一个相当大的性能差距，并且与这些类型的 IO 绑定操作一致。 在 Codename One 项目上为两者运行 mvn package 显示略低但仍然一致的改进。 我多次运行此程序以确保：

  • 工商管理硕士 M2 24gb - 18.346

这些不是中位数或平均值，只是一次特定执行的输出。 但我反复运行构建，数字与 M2 的大约 2 秒优势一致。 请注意，我使用的是 JDK 的 ARM 版本，而不是 x86 版本。

作为我工作的一部分，我还创建媒体和演示文稿。 我在 keynote 上做了很多工作，并从中导出内容。 下一个明显的测试是将我制作的小型演示文稿导出为 PDF 和电影。 在 PDF 导出中，我同时导出了构建的所有阶段。

  • 工商管理硕士 M2 24gb - 2.13

这再次显示了 M2 设备的健康优势。 但这是一个转折点，当导出到电影时，基准完全翻转，MBP 与 MBA 擦肩而过。

  • 工商管理硕士 M2 24gb - 39.59

解释

回想起来，这些数字不应该让我感到惊讶。 对于这些类型的负载，M2 会更快。 这不太可能用于当前测试，因此空气获胜。

空气流失的地方是 GPU 绑定工作。 我假设电影导出代码在 GPU 上完成所有编码，这在 M1 Max 上非常强大。 我希望这不会成为我的视频编辑工作的问题，但我想我会用我所拥有的来管理。

尽管该设备小了一英寸，但此时很难适应尺寸差异。 我过去曾在 MacBook Air 上工作过，所以我相信这会随着我的习惯而过去。 这是一个过程。 我对自己的决定感到很兴奋，在所有那些银色和灰色的 Mac 之后，黑色设备给人一种耳目一新的感觉。 电源砖也小得多，这是对常旅客来说非常重要的细节之一。

我为什么要使用 Mac？

这可能是显而易见的问题。 我不使用 iPhone，所以我还不如像一个优秀的黑客一样得到一台 Linux 笔记本电脑。 我仍然在 Codename One 上开发东西，在这里我偶尔需要一台 Mac 来进行 iOS 相关的工作。 它不那么频繁，但它发生了。 第二个原因是我现在已经很习惯了。 我考虑回到 Linux 的原因之一就是 docker。 我喜欢 M1/2 芯片。 他们太棒了。 不幸的是，许多 docker 映像仅是 Intel 的，在设置任何复杂的东西时都很难使用。 随着 ARM 机器的发展，问题正在自行解决。 但我们还没有。

最后

是的，我知道。 这篇文章令人震惊：新机器比旧机器更快。 但请记住，M1 在所有方面都是顶级产品，而 Air 拥有一半的性能核心。 它更薄、无风扇并且重量减轻了大约 30%。 这对单代更新来说是惊人的。

令人惊讶的是，我认为 M2 在 MacBook Air 中对于大多数人来说已经足够强大了。 我想即使 M1 Max 的价格相同，我也会选择它。 它更好看。 它更轻。 大多数对我来说很重要的事情在广播中表现得更好。 它很小但也不算太小，而且屏幕非常棒。 我可以忍受所有这些。 它没有旧版本所具有的奇怪的 MBA 锋利边缘。 这是一台很棒的机器。 希望蜜月期结束后我也会有同样的感觉，所以如果你在 2023 年阅读这篇文章，请随时评论/联系我，我可能会有更多的见解。

我矛盾的一点是贴纸。 黑色饰面非常漂亮。 但是我想要贴纸。 我很难将它们从 M1 机器上移除。 太快了……



原文:

This is a shocker… I just switched laptops, I thought I was downgrading from the “top of the line” M1 Max with 64gb (14.1 inch version) to a “tiny” MacBook Air M2 with “only” 24gb of RAM. Turns out I was wrong. The new M2 seems to be noticeably faster for my use cases as a Java developer. I was at first shocked but in retrospect I guess this makes sense.

I recently left my job at Lightrun. I usually buy my own laptops as I don’t enjoy constantly switching devices when I’m at work or working on personal things. But since I worked at Lightrun for so long I accepted their offer for a laptop. One year after I got the new laptop I found myself leaving the company. So arguably this was a big mistake. Turns out it wasn’t.

I wanted to buy the same machine. I was very pleased with the M1 Max. It’s powerful, fast, light and has a battery that lasts forever. It runs cool and looks cool. I placed an order with a local vendor who had the worst possible service. Ended up canceling that. Then I started looking around.

I initially dismissed the MacBook Airs. I had a couple of MacBook Airs in the past, they were good for some things. But I do a lot of video editing nowadays and I also can’t stand their sharp edges. They are uncomfortable to hold in some situations. The new MacBook airs finally increased the RAM. Not to 32gb as I’d have wanted but 24gb is already a vast improvement over the miniscule 16gb of older devices. They also come in black and cost much less than the equivalent pro. MacBook Airs are lighter and thinner. I’m very mobile, both because I travel, and because I work everywhere. For me the thin and light device is a tremendous advantage. You can check out the comparison tool on Apples website.

They do have two big disadvantages:

  • No HDMI port - that sucks a bit. It was nice plugging in at conferences with no dongle. But it’s not something I do often and AirPlay works for most other use cases.
  • Only one external monitor - I don’t use external monitors when I work so this wasn’t a problem for me. If you’re the type that needs two monitors for work then this isn’t the laptop for you.

Since both aren’t an issue for me and the other benefits are great. I saved some money and bought the air. I expected to take a performance hit. Turns out I got a major performance boost!

Migration

I used Time Machine to back up my older Mac and restore it to the new Mac. In terms of installed software and settings both devices should be identical. The stuff that is running on the old machine should be on the new machine as well. Including all the legacy that might be slowing it down. However, I wouldn’t consider my findings as scientific as this isn’t a clean environment. Everything is based on my use cases. Professional sites have better benchmarks for the common use cases. I suggest referring to them for a more complete picture.

However, for me the machine is MUCH better. Probably most glaring is the IDE startup. I use IntelliJ/IDEA Ultimate for most of my day to day work. I just started writing a new book after completing my last book (which you can preorder now), for that purpose I installed a fresh version of IntelliJ Community Edition. It doesn’t include all the plugins and setup in my typical install. It’s the perfect environment to check IDE startup time. Notice that I measured this with a stopwatch app, not ideal. I started the stopwatch with the icon click and stopped when the IDE fully loaded the Codename One project.

  • MBP M1 Max 64gb - 6.30
  • MBA M2 24gb - 4.54

This is a sizeable gap in performance and it’s consistent with these types of IO bound operations. Running mvn package on the Codename One project for both showed slightly lower but still consistent improvements. I ran this multiple times to make sure:

  • MBP M1 Max 64gb - 20.211
  • MBA M2 24gb - 18.346

These are not medians or averages, just the output of one specific execution. But I ran the build repeatedly and numbers were consistent with a rough 2 second advantage to the M2. Notice I used the ARM build of the JDK and not the x86 versions.

As part of my work, I also create media and presentations. I work a lot in keynote and export content from it. The next obvious test is to export a small presentation I made to both PDF and a movie. In the PDF export, I made both export all the stages of the build.

  • MBP M1 Max 64gb - 2.8
  • MBA M2 24gb - 2.13

Again this shows a healthy advantage to the M2 device. But here’s the twist, when exporting to a movie the benchmark flipped completely and the MBP wiped the floor with the MBA.

  • MBP M1 Max 64gb - 26.8
  • MBA M2 24gb - 39.59

Explanation

In retrospect, these numbers shouldn’t have surprised me. The M2 would be faster for these sorts of loads. IO would be faster. The only point where the M1 would gain an advantage would be if the 24gb of the air would be depleted. This isn’t likely for the current test so the air wins.

Where the air loses is in GPU bound work. I’m assuming the movie export code does all the encoding on the GPU which is huge and powerful on the M1 Max. I hope this won't be a problem for my video editing work but I guess I’ll manage with what I have.

Even though the device is smaller by one Inch only the size difference is hard to get used to at this point. I worked on a MacBook Air in the past so I’m sure this will pass as I get used to it. It’s a process. I’m thrilled with my decision and the black device is such a refreshing feeling after all of those silver and gray Macs. The power brick is also much smaller which is one of those details that matters so much to frequent travelers.

Why am I Using a Mac?

This might be the obvious question. I don’t use an iPhone, so I might as well get a Linux laptop like a good hacker. I still develop things on Codename One, here I occasionally need a Mac for iOS related work. It’s not as often, but it happens. The second reason is that I’m pretty used to it by now. The desktop on Linux still feels not as productive to me.

There is one reason I considered going back to Linux, and that’s docker. I love the M1/2 chips. They are fantastic. Unfortunately, many docker images are Intel only and that’s pretty hard to work with when setting up anything sophisticated. The problem is solving itself as ARM machines gain traction. But we aren’t there yet.

Finally

Yes i know. This article is shocking: newer machine faster than older machine. But keep in mind that the M1 was top of the line in all regards and the Air has half the performance cores. It's much thinner, fanless and is around 30% lighter. That's amazing over a single generation update.

Amazingly I think the M2 is powerful enough in a MacBook Air for most people. I think I would pick it even if the M1 Max was in the same price point. It’s better looking. It’s lighter. Most of the things that matter to me perform better on the air. It’s small but not too small and the screen is pretty great. I can live with all of those. It doesn’t have that weird MBA sharp edge older versions have. It’s a great machine. Hopefully, I’ll feel the same way when the honeymoon period is over, so if you’re reading this in 2023 feel free to comment/ping me, I might have additional insights.

The one point I’m conflicted about is stickers. The black finish is so pretty. But I want stickers. I had such a hard time removing them from the M1 machine. It’s too soon…

Items.Title: 十大服务 Apache APISIX 用例 | Top 10 services Apache APISIX use-cases
Items.Description:
您是否想知道什么是Apache APISIX以及谁在使用它？ 那么，这篇博文适合您。

全球各大洲的许多公司和组织都在使用 APISIX 进行研究和生产中的商业产品。

在本文中，我们列出了使用 Apache APISIX 管理其关键任务和大规模 API 流量的前 10 家公司和服务。

腾讯云是一种安全、可靠、高性能的云计算服务。 截至目前，腾讯已为腾讯内部十余个团队落地Apache APISIX，每日海量业务请求流量超过十亿。 同时，团队还为Apache APISIX开发了服务发现、RPC协议转换、与监控平台对接等十余项功能。 腾讯团队仍在为腾讯维护 Apache APISIX 的特性，并与 Apache APISIX 社区保持合作。

作为一家全球金融科技公司， Airwallex已搭建起全球金融平台，其支付网络覆盖全球130多个国家和地区的50多种货币。 在这些复杂的业务场景中，Airwallex选择了Apache APISIX做智能路由方案，由Apache APISIX网关决定数据应该在哪里处理。

微博是一个中文微博网站。 它是中国最大的社交媒体平台之一，每月活跃用户超过 5.82 亿。 它在引擎盖下使用 APISIX 作为基于云的微服务 API 网关来进行资源路由、处理身份验证和安全性，并管理每天来自 2.52 亿活跃用户的高流量。

它是全球智能手机行业排名前 10 的智能手机制造商之一。 其中许多提供了一个或多个 API，可供其他外部工具和服务或欧洲的开发人员使用。 通过使用 Apache APISIX API 网关，EFPF 平台可以监视、控制和分析可用 API 的使用情况，以更好地了解它们的使用方式。

哥白尼是欧盟的地球观测计划，旨在观察我们的星球及其环境，以造福所有欧洲公民。 它提供从卫星地球观测站获取数据的信息服务。 所提供的信息服务对用户免费开放。 最后但同样重要的是，APISIX 将被中央电视台（中国中央电视台）用于在中国直播卡塔尔 2022 年世界杯足球赛。 数十亿人将观看高质量的足球比赛。

概括

我们展示了一些受益于 APISIX 的服务和研究项目的简短列表，您可以在APISIX 网站上阅读更多关于其他成功案例的信息。

相关资源

推荐内容💁

十大服务 Apache APISIX 用例。

社区⤵️



原文:

Are you wondering what’s Apache APISIX, and who uses it? Well, this blog post is for you.

Apache APISIX is a lightweight, fast, independently deployable, highly-scalable, and open-source API Management solution. Apache APISIX is one of the fastest-growing top projects of the Apache Software Foundation like Apache Kafka, Spark, Cassandra, Hadoop, Tomcat, Camel, and so on.

A wide variety of companies and organizations use APISIX across every continent of the globe for research and commercial products in production.

In this post, we made a list of the TOP 10 companies and services that use Apache APISIX to manage their mission-critical and large-scale API traffic.

1. Tencent Cloud

Tencent Cloud is a secure, reliable, and high-performance cloud computing service. As of now, Tencent has landed Apache APISIX for more than ten teams within Tencent, with massive daily business request traffic exceeding one billion. At the same time, the team has also developed more than ten features for Apache APISIX, including Service Discovery, RPC Protocol Conversion, and connection with the monitoring platform. Tencent teams are still maintaining Apache APISIX’s features for Tencent and keeping cooperating with the Apache APISIX community.

2. Airwallex

As a global fintech company, Airwallex has built a global financial platform, with its payment network covering more than 50 currencies in over 130 countries and regions worldwide. In these complex business scenarios, Airwallex chooses Apache APISIX to make an intelligent routing solution, and the Apache APISIX gateway decides where the data should be processed.

3. Zoom

Zoom, has become a popular meeting tool for home office, online teaching, and social scenarios. APISIX Ingress Controller plays an important role in Zoom’s continuous delivery pipeline, releasing the pressure on project management, application development, environment management, and middleware and infrastructure management.

4. WPS

WPS(Writer, Presentation, and Spreadsheets) is currently the largest domestic office software manufacturer, its products include a free all-in-one WPS Office suite that’s highly compatible with Microsoft Office, Kdocs, Docer, and so on. Apache APISIX at WPS is currently responsible for providing gateway services to the mid-stage business that handles tens of billions of API calls per day, with peak QPS (Queries-per-second) exceeding 1.2 million.

5. Trendyol

Trendyol is an e-commerce platform based in Turkey, with its headquarters located in Istanbul. Trendyol is making use of many APISIX plugins to enable rich traffic management features and the Trendyol engineering team contributes regularly to new Plugin development.

6. Weibo

Weibo is a Chinese microblogging website. It is one of the biggest social media platforms in China, with over 582 million monthly active users. It uses APISIX under the hood as a cloud-based micro-services API gateway to resource routing, handle authentication, and security, and manage the high traffic from 252 million active users daily.

7. Vivo

Vivo is a Chinese multinational technology company that designs and develops smartphones, smartphone accessories, software, and online services. It is among the top 10 smartphone makers in the global smartphone industry. Vivo also leverages the power of APISIX solutions to efficiently and securely connect any API with different communication protocols.

8. European eFactory Platform

The EFPF federation offers a range of tools and services. Many of these provide one or more API’s that may be used by other external tools and services or by developers in Europe. Through the use of Apache APISIX API Gateway, the EFPF platform can monitor, control and analyze usage of the APIs available to better understand how they are used.

9. Copernicus Reference System Software

Copernicus is the European Union's Earth observation program, looking at our planet and its environment to benefit all European citizens. It offers information services that draw data from the satellite Earth Observatory. The information services provided are free and openly accessible to users. Copernicus considers the ingress controller APISIX as the best fit for their Reference System infrastructure project.

10. CCTV

Last but not least, APISIX will be used by CCTV (China Central Television) for live streaming of the Qatar FIFA World Cup 2022 in China. Billions of people are going to watch football matches with high quality.

Summary

We have shown a short list of some services and research programs that take benefit from APISIX, you can read more about other success stories on the APISIX website.

Items.Title: 在 AWS 上的 Kubernetes 集群上部署 React 应用程序的步骤 | Steps to Deploy a React app on a Kubernetes Cluster on AWS
Items.Description:
在本文中，我们将使用 Microtica 的生产就绪模板在 AWS 上的 Kubernetes 集群上部署 ReactJS 应用程序。

为什么要在 Kubernetes 上部署 React？

以下是为什么在 Kubernetes 上部署 React 应用程序是个好主意的一些原因：

  • 高级功能，例如金丝雀部署、蓝绿部署、零停机部署等。
  • 自动缩放使处理流量高峰成为可能
  • 水平扩展以最大限度地减少停机时间
  • 通过在不同的容器集上运行两个发布版本进行 A/B 测试

入门

要在 AWS 上部署 React，您需要以下模板：

  • 一键部署

    单击下面的使用 Microtica 部署并按照模板步骤在您的 AWS 账户上部署 ReactJS 应用程序。

    先决条件

    注册 Microtica 后，您将看到入职屏幕，在那里您可以找到 ReactJS 模板。 单击Deploy app按钮将带您进入 Templates 页面 - Microtica 的生产就绪模板列表。 您还可以在那里找到 ReactJS 模板并选择它。

    第一步是连接一个 Git 帐户。 连接首选提供商后，您将看到该部分更改为创建存储库，您的 ReactJS 应用程序代码将驻留在其中。

    我们将使用提供的名称在您的帐户上创建一个新的存储库，并且每个 Git 推送都将自动部署在 Microtica 中。

    这个模板是用Create React App引导的。

    如果您已经拥有 ReactJS 应用程序源代码，请按照如何在 Microtica 中导入现有应用程序的指南进行操作。

    创建存储库后，您需要在 Microtica 中为您的应用程序命名，以便能够将其与“应用程序”选项卡中的其他应用程序区分开来。

    您可以使用环境变量自定义此模板以满足您的要求。 您可以稍后在App Settings中更新配置。

    在第三步中，您可以选择您的应用程序将部署到的 Kubernetes 集群。 在您的 AWS 账户上部署意味着您需要创建一个 AWS 托管 Kubernetes (EKS) 集群，您可以通过单击“创建 Amazon EKS 集群”按钮来启动该过程。

    我们建议在 AWS 上部署您的生产应用程序，因为它可以让您完全拥有您的云基础设施和应用程序。

    单击创建 Amazon EKS 集群按钮将打开如上图所示的弹出窗口。 在此处选择要部署此集群的环境。 如果您没有现有环境，则可以通过从下拉菜单中选择创建环境操作来创建一个。

    选择环境后，您需要选择要部署EKS集群的AWS账户和地域。 如果您还没有连接 AWS 账户，您可以在此处点击连接 AWS 账户按钮进行连接。 这将启动一个 CloudFormation 堆栈，该堆栈将授予 Microtica 在您的 AWS 帐户上配置基础设施的权限。 创建堆栈后，连接将自动建立，您将能够在帐户下拉列表中看到 AWS 帐户。

    选择帐户，选择区域，然后单击“继续”以移动到您的环境部署。

    选择集群后，您将看到所有先前步骤的摘要以及将使用此模板部署的内容。

    单击Deploy按钮将触发环境部署并开始创建基础设施（EKS 集群），将其部署到您的 AWS 账户，然后在该基础设施上部署应用程序。

    在新的 EKS 集群上部署 React 应用程序大约需要 15-20 分钟。

    您可以通过单击“查看部署”按钮来关注详细的实时部署日志。

    完成新 EKS 集群的部署后，单击查看日志按钮以跟踪 React 应用程序的部署过程。

    部署完成后，您将看到一个查看应用程序按钮，引导您进入应用程序概述选项卡。

    由于您部署在 AWS 上的 EKS 集群上，您需要将您的应用程序公开到互联网，因此请按照本指南启用对您的应用程序的外部访问。

    有关更多信息，您还可以查看“部署”和“监控”选项卡，其中包含管道历史记录、资源消耗指标和实例日志。

    要了解有关如何在 Microtica 中扩展 ReactJS 应用程序的更多信息，请阅读以下有关在 Microtica 中扩展应用程序的文章。

    奖金：清理

    要在 Microtica 中取消部署您的应用程序，请导航到该应用程序，在应用程序设置 → 危险区域中，然后删除该应用程序。

    要删除在您的 AWS 帐户上创建的所有资源，请导航到您的环境（在我们的示例中为演示）→ Environment Settings → Infrastructure ，您将看到_Undeploy _部分。

    这将清理在 AWS 中创建的资源，但您仍将拥有 Microtica 中的配置，以防您想要再次将其部署到云中。



原文:

React is one of the most popular, most used, and according to The State of Frontend report, the most loved frontend framework by developers. In this article we’ll deploy a ReactJS application on a Kubernetes cluster on AWS, using Microtica’s production-ready templates.

Why deploy React on Kubernetes?

Here are some of the reasons why it would be a good idea to deploy your React application on Kubernetes:

  • Advanced features, such as canary deployments, blue-green deployments, zero-downtime deployments etc.
  • Auto-scaling makes it possible to handle peaks in traffic
  • Horizontal scaling to minimize downtime
  • A/B testing by running two release versions on separate container sets

Getting started

To deploy React on AWS, you need the following templates:

  • Amazon EKS
  • ReactJS This will setup a production-ready solution in less than 20 minutes.

One-click Deploy

Click Deploy with Microtica below and follow the template steps to deploy your ReactJS application on your AWS account.

Prerequisites

1. Create Git Repository

Once you signup for Microtica, you’ll be presented with the Onboarding screen, and there you can find the ReactJS template. Clicking the Deploy app button will lead you to the Templates page - a list of Microtica’s production-ready templates. There you can also find the ReactJS template and select it.

The first step is connecting a Git account. Microtica supports the major Git providers, like GitHub, Bitbucket, GitLab and CodeCommit. Once you connect your preferred provider, you’ll see the section change into creating a repository where your ReactJS app code will reside.

Microtica defines a repo name by default for each template, but you can change it. We will create a new repository on your account with the provided name, and every Git push will be deployed automatically in Microtica.

This template is bootstrapped with Create React App.

If you already have a ReactJS application source code, follow the guide on How to import your existing application in Microtica.

2. Customize the Template

After creating your repository, you need to give your application a name in Microtica, to be able to distinguish it from other apps in the Applications tab.

You can customize this template to meet your requirements with environment variables. You can update the configuration later in App Settings.

3. Create an EKS cluster

In step number three, you can choose the Kubernetes cluster where your application will deploy. Deploying on your AWS account means you need to create an AWS managed-Kubernetes (EKS) cluster, and you can start that process by clicking on the Create Amazon EKS Cluster button.

We recommend deploying your production applications on AWS, as it gives you full ownership of your cloud infrastructure and applications.

Clicking the Create Amazon EKS Cluster button will open the popup shown in the image above. Here you select the environment in which you want this cluster to be deployed. If you don't have an existing environment, you can create one by selecting the Create Environment action from the dropdown menu.

After selecting an environment, you need to select an AWS account and region where the EKS cluster will be deployed. If you haven’t connected an AWS account yet, you can do it right here by clicking the Connect AWS Account button. This will initiate a CloudFormation stack that will give Microtica permissions to provision infrastructure on your AWS account. Once you create the stack, the connection will be established automatically and you'll be able to see the AWS account in the dropdown list of accounts.

Select the account, select a region, and then click on Continue to move to the deployment of your environment.

4. Deploying the ReactJS app to Kubernetes

Once you’ve selected the cluster, you will see a summary of all the previous steps and what will be deployed with this template.

Clicking the Deploy button will trigger a deployment of the environment and start creating the infrastructure (the EKS cluster), deploying it on your AWS account, and then deploying the application on that infrastructure.

It will take approximately 15-20 mins for the React app to be deployed on a new EKS cluster.

You can follow the detailed real-time deployment logs by clicking the View deployment button.

When the deployment of a new EKS cluster is finished, click the View logs button to follow the deployment process of the React app.

5. Access the application

When the deployment is finished, you will see a View Application button leading you to the app Overview tab.

Since you deployed on your EKS cluster on AWS, you need to expose your application to the internet, so follow this guide to enable external access to your apps.

For even more information, you can also look at the Deployments and Monitoring tabs, where you have the pipeline history, resource consumption metrics, and logs for your instances.

To learn more about how to scale your ReactJS application in Microtica, read the following article about Scaling Applications in Microtica.

Bonus: Cleanup

To undeploy your application in Microtica, navigate to the app, in App Settings → Danger Zone, and delete the application.

To remove all the resources created on your AWS account, navigate to your environment (demo in our example) → Environment Settings → Infrastructure, and you’ll see the _Undeploy _section.

This will clean up the resources created in AWS, but you will still have the configuration in Microtica in case you want to deploy it in the cloud again.

Items.Description:
所以在https://bookmarksy.io开发的第 2 周结束时，我现在有一个（半）功能的前端和后端！

我将在以后的帖子中对每个组件进行技术分解，但我想花一些时间谈谈我在集成 Twitter 身份验证时遇到的挑战。

最初，我花了一些时间熟悉 Twitter 为应用程序客户端公开的授权和身份验证流程。

我正在使用的应用程序客户端类型意味着我最好的授权选择是带有 PKCE 的 OAuth2.0。

问：什么是带有 PKCE 的 OAuth 2？

答：OAuth 2.0 是行业标准的授权协议。 与 PKCE（用于代码交换的证明密钥）配对，它有助于防止 CSRF（跨站点请求伪造）和授权代码注入攻击。

这是流程图：

一个典型的用户故事看起来像这样：

最初，我尝试用物理端点手动实现它，一段时间后效果很好，但在偶然发现 twitter API SDK 之后，我找到了一个更优雅的解决方案https://github.com/twitterdev/twitter-api -打字稿SDK 。

所以现在代码看起来像这样：

 // create an oauth2 client const authClient = new auth . OAuth2User ({ client_id : process . env . CLIENT_ID , client_secret : process . env . CLIENT_SECRET , callback : " http://127.0.0.1:3000/callback " , scopes : [ " tweet.read " , " users.read " , " offline.access " ], }); const client = new Client ( authClient ); // redirect client to this url const authUrl = authClient . generateAuthURL ({ code_challenge_method : " s256 " , }); // after authorization, retrieve code from query param await authClient . requestAccessToken ( code ); // revoke token const response = await authClient . revokeAccessToken ();

效果很好！

我目前唯一担心的是revokeAccessToken不使用解析的访问令牌，所以我不确定它究竟如何知道要撤销对哪个令牌的访问以及这是否会影响多个用户。

原文:

So at the end of week 2 of https://bookmarksy.io ‘s development, I now have a (semi) functioning frontend and backend!

I’ll do a technical breakdown of each component in a future post but would like to spend some time talking about the challenges I faced integration twitter authentication.

Initially, I spent some time familiarizing myself with the authorization and authentication flows twitter exposes for app clients.

The type of app client I’m working with means my best option for authorization is OAuth2.0 with PKCE.

Q. What is OAuth 2 with PKCE?

A. OAuth 2.0 is the industry-standard protocol for authorization. Paired with PKCE (Proof key for code exchange), it helps prevent CSRF (Cross Site Request Forgery) and authorization code injection attacks.

Here’s a diagram of the flow:

A typical user story would look something like this:

The web app can revoke an access token like so:

curl --location --request POST 'https://api.twitter.com/2/oauth2/revoke' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' \
--header 'Authorization: Basic {SOME AUTH TOKEN FOR YOUR APP}'\
--data-urlencode 'token={SOME ACCESS TOKEN TO REVOKE}'

Initially, I tried to implement this by hand with the physical endpoints, which worked fine after some time, but after stumbling upon the twitter API SDK, I found a much more elegant solution https://github.com/twitterdev/twitter-api-typescript-sdk.

So now the code looks something like this:

// create an oauth2 client
const authClient = new auth.OAuth2User({ client_id: process.env.CLIENT_ID, client_secret: process.env.CLIENT_SECRET, callback: "http://127.0.0.1:3000/callback", scopes: ["tweet.read", "users.read", "offline.access"],
}); const client = new Client(authClient); // redirect client to this url
const authUrl = authClient.generateAuthURL({ code_challenge_method: "s256",
}); // after authorization, retrieve code from query param
await authClient.requestAccessToken(code); // revoke token
const response = await authClient.revokeAccessToken();

Which works wonderfully!

My only concern at the moment is that the revokeAccessToken doesn’t use a parsed access token, so I’m unsure as to how exactly it knows which token to revoke access to and whether this will impact multiple users.

But otherwise, https://twitter.com/bookmarksy_io (https://bookmarksy.io) is coming along well!

Follow the journey on twitter bookmarksy_io or brandonkpbailey and join the waitlist! https://brandonkpbailey.substack.com/

Items.Description:
每个星期二，我们都会根据流量、参与度和一些编辑策划来汇总前一周的热门帖子。 典型的一周从星期一开始到星期日结束，但不用担心，我们会考虑本周晚些时候发布的帖子。

编码前要知道的 10 件事

你还记得你第一次决定学习编码是什么时候吗？ 兴奋、惊奇……完全不知所措的感觉？ 像专业人士一样在 React 中渲染列表

我们经常被要求在 React 面试中呈现一个列表。 在这篇文章中， @andyrewlee着眼于一个基本的实现，并提出了四种我们可以改进它以使其脱颖而出的方法。

使用 Docz 构建文档

如果文档不够好，将很难学习如何使用它。 以下是如何使用 Docz 为您的下一个项目准备文档！

日常使用编程工具

因此，她创建了一个列表，列出了她最喜欢的所有工具，这些工具可以帮助您提高工作效率并帮助您实现想要实现的目标。

如何应对失去工作

如果您经历过同样的事情，您可能会感到类似的失望。 阅读她的故事，看看在发生如此大的事情之后接下来的步骤会是什么样子。

重构初级代码

作为一名（有抱负的）初级 React 开发人员，很难理解你的代码是否优秀。 分享您的代码需要勇气，而您在在线社区中获得的评论通常很肤浅。 我的 GitHub 存储库上有 1200 多颗星！

遵循这四个基本步骤可以大大提高 GH 存储库的可见性。

这就是我们本周二的每周前 7 名！ 请关注本周的 dev.to 以获取每日内容和讨论……并确保在未来关注本系列。 你可能只是在其中！



原文:

Every Tuesday we round up the previous week's top posts based on traffic, engagement, and a hint of editorial curation. The typical week starts on Monday and ends on Sunday, but don't worry, we take into account posts that are published later in the week.

10 Things to Know Before Coding

Do you remember when you first decided to learn to code? The excitement, the wonder... the feeling of utter cluelessness? @marisabrantley explains her experience learning to code and how to pick through all the advice!

Render Lists in React like a Pro

We often get asked to render a list in a React interview. In this article, @andyrewlee looks at a basic implementation and comes up with four ways we can improve it to stand out from the rest.

Building Documentation using Docz

@surajondev uses a tool’s documentation to know whether or not to add it to a project. If the documentation isn’t good enough, it’s going to be hard to learn how to use it. Here’s how to prep documentation for your next project using Docz!

Daily Use Programming Tools

@yuridevat gets asked a lot about what kind of tools she uses on a daily basis and why. So, she created a list of all her favorite tools which can help make you more productive and help you reach the goals you want to achieve.

How to Cope with Losing your Job

On June 30, @supeergabs_ lost her job. You might have felt a similar kind of disappointment if you’ve gone through the same thing. Read her story and see what the next steps might look like after something this big.

Refactoring a Junior’s Code

As an (aspiring) Junior React developer it’s hard to understand if your code is any good. Sharing your code takes guts and the reviews you get in online communities are often shallow. @jkettmann shares his experience reviewing the code of a junior developer.

1200+ Stars on my GitHub repo!

@fredy wants to share some tips & strategies that helped his repo make it to 1200+ stars! Following these four basic steps can do a lot to improve the visibility of your GH repo.

That's it for our weekly Top 7 for this Tuesday! Keep an eye on dev.to this week for daily content and discussions...and be sure to keep an eye on this series in the future. You might just be in it!

Items.Title: 使用 MD4 时出现 OpenSSL::Digest::DigestError | OpenSSL::Digest::DigestError when using MD4
Items.Description:
亚历克斯·安齐费罗夫

发表于在我正在使用的其中一个应用程序中将 Ruby 升级到 3.1.0 后遇到了这个问题。

我正在使用rbenv来管理 Ruby 版本，结果他们最近开始使用 OpenSSL 3.0 而不是 OpenSSL 1.1 构建 Ruby 。

反过来，OpenSSL 3.0已经淘汰了许多算法（包括该应用程序中使用的 MD4 函数）。

你好！ 更新后，我们调用OpenSSL::Digest::MD4.hexdigest()并得到：
OpenSSL::Digest::DigestError, message: Digest initialization failed: initialization error

如果您不能立即摆脱 MD4，则有一个临时解决方法：

  • 使用 OpenSSL 1.1 安装 Ruby（注意我使用的是 MacOS/brew）： export RUBY_CONFIGURE_OPTS="--with-openssl-dir=$(brew --prefix openssl@1.1)" && rbenv install 3.1.0
  • 作为替代方案，考虑在 OpenSSL 中重新启用 MD4（这看起来很有用）

Came across this issue after upgrading Ruby in one of the apps I'm working on to 3.1.0.

I'm using rbenv to manage Ruby versions, and it turns out they recently started building Ruby with OpenSSL 3.0 instead of OpenSSL 1.1.

And OpenSSL 3.0, in turn, has retired a number of algorithms (including MD4 function, which was used in that app).

And hello! After the update, we call OpenSSL::Digest::MD4.hexdigest() and get this:
OpenSSL::Digest::DigestError, message: Digest initialization failed: initialization error

  • install Ruby with OpenSSL 1.1 (note I'm using MacOS/brew): export RUBY_CONFIGURE_OPTS="--with-openssl-dir=$(brew --prefix openssl@1.1)" && rbenv install 3.1.0
  • as an alternative, look into re-enabling MD4 in OpenSSL (this looks useful)

Items.Title: A/B 测试对小型网站的影响很小 | Small effects of A/B testing on small websites
Items.Description:
我在工作中做过 A/B 测试。 做完之后的感受是A/B testing是非常精细和复杂的，所以很难搞定。 我认为失败的可能性很大。 在这篇文章中，我想讨论为什么 A/B 测试如此困难。

例如，目标是页面转换、按钮点击、转换等。这应该根据进行 A/B 测试的位置进行适当设置。

需要注意的是，A/B 测试只能告诉我们什么是可能的，并不意味着我们真正发布产品时结果总是会更好。

既然我给了你一个概述，我就说说为什么这个A/B测试这么难。 首先，让我们谈谈目标设定。 例如，假设您要测试将您带到表单的链接，您将链接被按下的次数设置为目标值。 在这一点上，需要注意的是，即使在模式 B 中链接被按下的次数更多，也并不一定意味着完成的转化次数会更高。 在 A/B 测试中，您不想通过更改来降低的业务关键指标（例如已完成的转换数）称为护栏指标。

实际发布在 A/B 测试中表现良好但不影响转换或产生负面影响的模式的一个可能原因是忽略了此护栏指标。

当然，最好将最终目标，即转化完成率，作为A/B测试的目标，但也可能会出现转化人群太少无法测试的情况，因为目标的差异没有显着可比性. 这就是护栏指标发挥作用的地方，这最终与将转换完成率设置为 A/B 测试目标相同。 换句话说，如果流入和转换的数量相当低，A/B 测试将不起作用。

我相信这种现象通常发生在站点更改本身开始时。 对于目标明确的网站，例如着陆页，您应该设计用户如何实现目标的故事。 从宏观角度来看，这是非常重要的。

如果流入量和转化率较低，我们认为对故事进行更改更有效，例如减少页面或更丰富的主视觉，而不是按钮装饰或措辞等小改动。



原文:

I have done A/B testing in my work. My impression after doing it is that A/B testing is very delicate and intricate, so it is very difficult to handle. I think there is a high possibility of failure. In this article, I would like to discuss why A/B testing is so difficult.

A/B testing is a method of testing two sites by delivering a pattern of appearance and functionality to a specified percentage of users, to see which pattern is more likely to achieve a given goal. The goals are, for example, page transitions, button clicks, conversions, etc. This is expected to be set appropriately depending on where the A/B test is conducted.

It is important to note that A/B testing can only tell us what is likely, it does not mean that the results will always be better when we actually release the product.

Now that I have given you an overview, I will talk about why this A/B testing is so difficult. First of all, let's talk about goal setting. For example, let's say you want to test a link that takes you to a form, and you set the number of times the link is pressed as the target value. At this point, it is important to note that even if the link is pressed more often in Pattern B, it does not necessarily mean that the number of completed conversions will be higher. In A/B testing, the business-critical metrics that you do not want to lower by making changes, such as the number of completed conversions, are called guardrail metrics.

A possible reason for the actual release of a pattern that performed well in A/B testing but did not affect conversions or had a negative impact is that this guardrail metric is being ignored.

Of course, it is best to set the final goal, the conversion completion rate, as the target for A/B testing, but there may be cases where the population of conversions is too small to test because the differences in goals are not significantly comparable. This is where guardrail metrics come into play, which is ultimately the same as setting the conversion completion rate as the A/B test goal. In other words, A/B testing will not work if the number of inflows and conversions are considerably low.

I believe this phenomenon often occurs with site changes themselves to begin with. For a website with a clear goal, such as an landing page, you should design the story of how the user achieves the goal. This is very important from a macro perspective.

If the number of inflows and conversions are low, we believe it is more effective to make changes to the story, such as fewer pages or a richer main visual, rather than small changes such as button decorations or wording.

Items.Title: AWS EventBridge 无服务器事件总线 | AWS EventBridge the serverless event bus
Items.Description:
拉什万·拉兹卡尼

发表于好的，让我们谈谈 Amazon EventBridge！

什么是亚马逊事件桥？

它还支持 AWS 服务、您自己的服务和不同的 SaaS 提供商之间的集成。

为什么选择 Amazon EventBridge？

  • 它是无服务器的，意味着您只需为使用的内容付费
  • 比SNS/SQS更多的可能性
  • 创建自己的过滤器
  • 支持超过 42 个 Amazon EventBridge SaaS 应用程序集成
  • 容易上手！

事件结构

以下事件显示了来自 Amazon EC2 的简单 AWS 事件（来源： AWS ）：

 { "version" : "0" , "id" : "6a7e8feb-b491-4cf7-a9f1-bf3703467718" , "detail-type" : "EC2 Instance State-change Notification" , "source" : "aws.ec2" , "account" : "111122223333" , "time" : "2017-12-22T18:43:48Z" , "region" : "us-west-1" , "resources" : [ "arn:aws:ec2:us-west-1:123456789012:instance/i-1234567890abcdef0" ], "detail" : { "instance-id" : "i-1234567890abcdef0" , "state" : "terminated" }
}

规则

让我们看一些示例规则。

假设您只想获取源aws.ec2的所有事件。

 { "source": ["aws.ec2"] }

或者您可能想要匹配来自 Amazon EC2 或 AWS Fargate 的事件：

 { "source": ["aws.ec2", "aws.fargate"] }

高级规则

您也有可以使用的高级规则。 例如假设以下 AWS 事件（来源： AWS ）：

 { "Source" : "custom.myATMapp" , "EventBusName" : "default" , "DetailType" : "transaction" , "Time" : "Wed Jan 29 2020 08:03:18 GMT-0500" , "Detail" : { "action" : "withdrawal" , "location" : "NY-NYC-001" , "amount" : 300 , "result" : "approved" , "transactionId" : "123456" , "cardPresent" : true , "partnerBank" : "Example Bank" , "remainingFunds" : 722.34 }
}

金额超过 $300 的交易：

 { "source" : [ "custom.myATMapp" ], "detail-type" : [ "transaction" ], "detail" : { "amount" : [ { "numeric" : [ ">" , 300 ] } ] }
}

纽约市的所有 ATM：

 { "source" : [ "custom.myATMapp" ], "detail-type" : [ "transaction" ], "detail" : { "location" : [ { "prefix" : "NY-NYC-" } ] }
}

使用第三方银行账户的 ATM 客户：

 { "source" : [ "custom.myATMapp" ], "detail-type" : [ "transaction" ], "detail" : { "partnerBank" : [ { "exists" : true } ] }
}

组合过滤器：您还可以使用强大的组合过滤器来创建更复杂的用例。 

 { "source" : [ "custom.myATMapp" ], "detail-type" : [ "transaction" ], "detail" : { "result" : [ "approved" ], "partnerBank" : [ { "exists" : false } ], "location" : [ { "anything-but" : "NY-NYC-002" }] }
}

值得考虑的一件好事是为每个订阅者使用一个规则。

概括

内置多个目标，例如 API 目的地、API 网关、CloudWatch 日志组、EC2、Kinesis 流、Lambda 函数、SNS 主题、SQS 队列和 Step Functions 状态机。 为传入事件创建规则，并将它们发送到您的特定目标，以便以非常流畅的方式进行处理。 总之，真的值得一试！

欢迎任何意见、问题或讨论！

原文:

Rashwan Lazkani

Posted on Nov 15

Okey so let's talk about Amazon EventBridge!

What is Amazon EventBridge?

Amazon EventBridge is a serverless, fully managed and scalable event bus that lets you receive, filter, transform, route, and deliver events. It also enables integrations between AWS services, your own services and different SaaS providers.

EventBridge was formerly called Amazon CloudWatch Events. Amazon CloudWatch Events and EventBridge are the same underlying service and API, however, EventBridge provides more features (source: AWS).

Why Amazon EventBridge?

  • It's serverless meaning that you only pay for what you use
  • More possibilities than SNS/SQS
  • Create your own filters
  • Supports over 42 Amazon EventBridge SaaS app integrations
  • Easy to get started with!

Event structure

The following event shows a simple AWS event from Amazon EC2 (source: AWS):

{ "version": "0", "id": "6a7e8feb-b491-4cf7-a9f1-bf3703467718", "detail-type": "EC2 Instance State-change Notification", "source": "aws.ec2", "account": "111122223333", "time": "2017-12-22T18:43:48Z", "region": "us-west-1", "resources": [ "arn:aws:ec2:us-west-1:123456789012:instance/i-1234567890abcdef0" ], "detail": { "instance-id": "i-1234567890abcdef0", "state": "terminated" }
}

Rules

Let's have a look of some example rules.

Assume you want to get all events with the source aws.ec2 only.

{
    "source": ["aws.ec2"]
}

Or maybe you want to match events from Amazon EC2 or AWS Fargate:

{
    "source": ["aws.ec2", "aws.fargate"]
}

Advanced rules

You do also have advanced rules that you can use. Assume the following AWS event for example (source: AWS):

{ "Source": "custom.myATMapp", "EventBusName": "default", "DetailType": "transaction", "Time": "Wed Jan 29 2020 08:03:18 GMT-0500", "Detail": { "action": "withdrawal", "location": "NY-NYC-001", "amount": 300, "result": "approved", "transactionId": "123456", "cardPresent": true, "partnerBank": "Example Bank", "remainingFunds": 722.34 }
}

Transactions where the amount is over $300:

{ "source": [ "custom.myATMapp" ], "detail-type": [ "transaction" ], "detail": { "amount": [ { "numeric": [ ">", 300 ] } ] }
}

All ATMs in New York City:

{ "source": [ "custom.myATMapp" ], "detail-type": [ "transaction" ], "detail": { "location": [ { "prefix": "NY-NYC-" } ] }
}

ATM customers using a third-party bank account:

{ "source": [ "custom.myATMapp" ], "detail-type": [ "transaction" ], "detail": { "partnerBank": [ { "exists": true } ] }
}

Combined filter: You can also use a powerful combine filter to create use-cases that are more complex.

{ "source": [ "custom.myATMapp" ], "detail-type": [ "transaction" ], "detail": { "result": [ "approved" ], "partnerBank": [ { "exists": false } ], "location": [ { "anything-but": "NY-NYC-002" }] }
}

A good thing to think about is to use a single rule per subscriber.

Summary

Amazon EventBridge make it easier for you to develop event-driven systems. There are multiple targets built in such as API destinations, API Gateway, CloudWatch log group, EC2, Kinesis stream, Lambda function, SNS topic, SQS queue and Step Functions state machine. Create your rules for the incoming events and send them to your specific targets for processing in a really smooth way. All in all, really worth a try!

Any comments, questions or discussions are always welcome!

Bhuvaneswari Subramani - Oct 10

Ganesh Swaminathan - Oct 30

Items.Title: 我正在合并 css-in-js 和 Tailwind | Im merging css-in-js and Tailwind
Items.Description:
桑娜贾梅

发表于我正在合并 css-in-js 和 Tailwind。 让我解释。

为什么在存在 twin.macro 时将 Tailwind 与 CSS-in-JS 合并？

原因很简单，有三个方面。 作为 Next.js 的长期用户，我已经非常喜欢 SWC 编译器。 正如它的所有用户现在所知，这意味着没有 babel 转换，因此没有twin.macro 。

第二个原因是可重用性。 创建一个可以通过 props 变化的按钮组件需要手动将 props 映射到 tailwind 类名。 这很快就会变得非常累人，并且很难用纯 Tailwind 创建 React UI 库。

第三个也是最后一个原因是提高我的 Typescript 知识。 创建 Typescript 库并不容易，要让它们推断 props，幕后的配置就更难了。 因此，我创建图书馆的旅程开始了。

要求

我列出了这个库的需求列表，这些是针对我在使用 Tailwind 开发 UI 库和 React 组件时所面临的挑战。

  1. 它必须能够合并多个类名
  2. 它必须支持根据输入切换不同的类名
  3. 它必须能够创建像button或span这样的 JSX
  4. 一流的 TypeScript 支持

灵感

当试图想出一个好的 API 时，我只需要朝一个方向看。 缝线.js 。 在我看来，这是迄今为止 JS 库中最好的 CSS，因此，它具有我想要创建的确切 API。 请检查他们的库并给予 Modulz/WorkOS 他们应得的荣誉！

介绍@tw-classed/react & @tw-classed/core public alpha

这些都处于alpha阶段，因为我仍在确定使它们工作所需的 Typescript 类型。 因此，我决定在测试时只在公共 alpha 版中发布这两个库。

完整的文档在这里。 下面是对这些库的简要介绍。

@tw-classed/core

这是 React 库的框架无关核心。 它可以在 vanilla JS 中独立使用来创建动态的 Tailwind 类（或者在不使用 React 时在其他库中使用）。 它只是充当类合并器和切换器，可以以任何可以想象的方式使用。

 import classed from " @tw-classed/core " ; const simpleButton = classed ( " flex items-center py-3 " , " hover:bg-gray-200 focus:bg-gray-200 " // Accepts any number of arguments ); console . log ( simpleButton ()); // => "flex items-center py-3 hover:bg-gray-200 focus:bg-gray-200" const button = classed ( " flex items-center " , // Apply your base styles { // Define your variants variants : { size : { sm : " py-2 px-4 " , md : " py-3 px-6 " , lg : " py-4 px-8 " , }, color : { primary : " bg-blue-500 text-white " , secondary : " bg-gray-500 text-white " , }, }, defaultVariants : { size : " md " , color : " primary " , }, } ); console . log ( button ()); // => "flex items-center py-3 px-6 bg-blue-500 text-white" console . log ( button ({ size : " sm " })); // => "flex items-center py-2 px-4 bg-blue-500 text-white" console . log ( button ({ size : " lg " , color : " secondary " })); // => "flex items-center py-4 px-8 bg-gray-500 text-white"

@tw-classed/react

这是 React 库。 它是@tw-classed/core的包装器，提供类似于styled-components或stitches.js的 React 组件创建器。

 import classed from " @tw-classed/react " ; const Button = classed ( " button " , " flex items-center " , { variants : { size : { sm : " py-2 px-4 " , md : " py-3 px-6 " , lg : " py-4 px-8 " , }, color : { primary : " bg-blue-500 text-white " , secondary : " bg-gray-500 text-white " , }, }, defaultVariants : { size : " md " , color : " primary " , }, }); const App = () => { return ( < div > < Button > Default </ Button > < Button size = "sm" > Small </ Button > < Button size = "lg" color = "secondary" > Large Secondary </ Button > </ div > ); };

在路上

在我有信心发布 V1 稳定版本之前，这个库还有很多事情要做。 请不要在生产中使用它。 这是未来事物的清单。

  • 类合并/差异（当变体覆盖时省略类名）
  • 打字稿性能测试
  • 稳定的core TypeScript react目前只有 React 是稳定的
  • 为智能感知改进 TailwindCSS 扩展类 RegEx

脚注

感谢您阅读本文！ 我希望许多人会测试这个库并提供反馈，因为它对改进至关重要。 欢迎任何 PR 和问题！

这是GitHub

可以在我的网站或通过Twitter 联系到我

查看您的总反应、视图和列表。

原文:

Sanna Jammeh

Posted on Nov 15

I'm merging css-in-js and Tailwind. Let me explain.

Why merge Tailwind with CSS-in-JS when twin.macro exists?

The reason is quite simple and threefold. As a long time user of Next.js I've grown quite fond of the SWC compiler. As all users of it know by now, that means no babel transforms and thus no twin.macro.

The second reason is reusability. Creating a button component which can vary via props requires manually mapping props to tailwind classnames. This gets very tiring very fast and makes it quite difficult to create React UI libraries with pure Tailwind.

The third and final reason is improve my Typescript knowledge. It's not easy creating Typescript libraries, making them infer props, configurations all under the hood is even harder. And thus, my journey creating the library began.

The requirements

I made a list of requirements for this library, these are specific to challenges I faced developing UI libraries and React components with Tailwind.

  1. It must be able to merge multiple classnames
  2. It must have support for toggling different classnames based on input
  3. It must be able to create JSX like button or span
  4. First class TypeScript support

Inspiration

When trying to come up with a good API I only had to look in one direction. Stitches.js. In my opinion, this is the best CSS in JS library made to date, and as such, it had the exact API I was looking to create. Please check their library out and give Modulz/WorkOS the credit they deserve!

Introducing @tw-classed/react & @tw-classed/core public alpha

These are both in alpha stages as I'm still ironing out the Typescript types required to make them work. The types in core are not stable enough for advanced usage just yet.

@tw-classed/react is quite stable even for advanced Typescript usage, though I haven't performed any tsc performance tests nor heavy testing just yet. As such I've decided to only release these two libraries in a public alpha while I'm testing.

The full documentation is here. Below is a tiny introduction to the libraries.

@tw-classed/core

This is the framework agnostic core of the react library. It can be used standalone in vanilla JS to create dynamic Tailwind classes (or in other libraries when not using react). It simply acts as a class merger and toggler and can be used in any way imaginable.

import classed from "@tw-classed/core"; const simpleButton = classed( "flex items-center py-3", "hover:bg-gray-200 focus:bg-gray-200" // Accepts any number of arguments
); console.log(simpleButton()); // => "flex items-center py-3 hover:bg-gray-200 focus:bg-gray-200" const button = classed( "flex items-center", // Apply your base styles { // Define your variants variants: { size: { sm: "py-2 px-4", md: "py-3 px-6", lg: "py-4 px-8", }, color: { primary: "bg-blue-500 text-white", secondary: "bg-gray-500 text-white", }, }, defaultVariants: { size: "md", color: "primary", }, }
); console.log(button()); // => "flex items-center py-3 px-6 bg-blue-500 text-white"
console.log(button({ size: "sm" })); // => "flex items-center py-2 px-4 bg-blue-500 text-white"
console.log(button({ size: "lg", color: "secondary" })); // => "flex items-center py-4 px-8 bg-gray-500 text-white"

@tw-classed/react

This is the React library. It's a wrapper around @tw-classed/core provides a React component creator similar to styled-components or stitches.js.

import classed from "@tw-classed/react"; const Button = classed("button", "flex items-center", { variants: { size: { sm: "py-2 px-4", md: "py-3 px-6", lg: "py-4 px-8", }, color: { primary: "bg-blue-500 text-white", secondary: "bg-gray-500 text-white", }, }, defaultVariants: { size: "md", color: "primary", },
}); const App = () => { return ( <div> <Button>Default</Button> <Button size="sm">Small</Button> <Button size="lg" color="secondary"> Large Secondary </Button> </div> );
};

Down the road

There is still lots of things to do with this library before I feel confident in releasing a V1 stable release. Please don't use it in production just yet. Here is a list of future things.

  • Class merger/differ (Omits classNames when a variant overrides)
  • compoundVariants - Stitches inspired API to toggle variants when other variants are defined
  • Typescript performance testing
  • Stable core typescript API - currently only react is stable
  • Improve TailwindCSS Extension class RegEx for intellisense

Footnote

Thank you for reading this article! I hope many will test the library and provide feedback as its essential to improving. Any PR's and issues are welcome!

Items.Title: 正则表达式简介 | Introduction to regex
Items.Description:
以色列洛佩斯

发表于正则表达式是一种指定文本模式的正式方法。

怎么会这样？

那就简单解释一下吧！

来一段文字吧，这段文字有一千多字，你要找
一个特定的词，不仅如此，它还想在出现时替换它。

正则表达式可以为您完成这项工作，定位单词并根据您建立的条件逻辑替换它们。

什么是旗帜

标志是决定我们的正则表达式行为方式的标记，它们决定我们的正则表达式的逻辑，什么将被过滤，什么将被忽略。

例子：

  • 标志 (g) 和全局标志，将在整个字符串中搜索。
  • 标志（i），忽略大小写。 忽略大写和小写字母。

如何使用旗帜

 const text = 'Rato roeu a roupa do rei de Roma'; const regex = /r/gi console.log(text.match(regex))

输出

 ['R', 'r', 'r', 'r', 'R']

在上面的这个例子中，使用了标志“ g ”和“ i ”。 在这里，它在整个字符串中搜索大写或小写字母，并在输出中注明。

检查空格

使用正则表达式获取空格的示例。

 console.log(' '.match(/\s/g))

单个字符

const text = '1,2,3,4,5,6.ab c!d?e' const regex = /,/ console.log(text.split(regex))

在此示例中，它将在有逗号的地方分隔我们的字符串并忽略它。 这个结果只有使用split()才有可能。

输出：

 ['1', '2', '3', '4', '5', '6.ab c!d?e']

元字符姓名意义观点任何一个角色（小丑） 放允许的字符集设置拒绝禁止字符集

元字符 - 量词

元字符姓名意义
可选的零或一
星号零个或多个
湿一个或多个
钥匙从 n 到 m

元字符 - 锚点

元字符姓名意义
抑扬音从大林哈开始
西弗劳菲姆达林哈
博尔达其他元字符
元字符姓名意义
逃脱将元字符与文字一起使用
或者
团体定义一个组
后视镜拯救已经定义的群体

使用通配符元字符：

 const text = '1,2,3,4,,5,6,7,8,9,0' console.logh(text.match(/1.2/g)) console.log(text.match(/1..2/g))

输出

在上面的这个例子中，它将在整个字符串中搜索一个从“1”开始到“2”结束的序列，但在中间我们有一个通配符，这意味着那里有什么字符并不重要。 在第二个中，它返回一个 null，因为 1 和 2 之间没有两个字符。

在字符串中搜索空格的方法

在此示例中，它将在“a”和“b”之间寻找 3 个空格。 下面我做了几种导致相同结果的方法。

 const text = 'ab' console.logh(text.match(/ab/)) console.log(text.match(/a\s\s\sb/)) console.log(text.match(/a {3}b/)) console.log(text.match(/a\s{3}b/)) console.log(text.match(/\s+b/))

让我们看看制作正则表达式的其他方法。

获取 AZ 范围内的所有字母。

 console.log(text.match(/[az]/g))

你取 BD 范围内的所有字母。

 console.log(text.match(/[bd]/g))

获取 2 到 4 范围内的所有数字。

 console.log(text.match(/[2-4]/g))

获取给定范围内的所有字母和数字

console.log(text.match(/[A-Z1-3]/g))

原文:

Israel-Lopes

Posted on Nov 15

Regular expression is a formal method of specifying a text pattern.

How so?

A brief explanation then!

Let's have a text, this text has more than a thousand words, you want to find
a specific word and not only that, it also wants to replace it whenever it occurs.

Regular expression can do this for you, locate the words and replace them according to a conditional logic established by you.

What are flags

Flags are markers that will determine how our regex will behave, they determine the logic of our regex, what will be filtered and what will be ignored.

Example:

  • flag (g) and the global flag, with it will be searched in the whole string.
  • flag(i), ignore case. Ignores uppercase and lowercase letters.

How to use the flags

 const text = 'Rato roeu a roupa do rei de Roma';

 const regex = /r/gi

 console.log(text.match(regex))

output

 ['R', 'r', 'r', 'r', 'R']

In this example above the flags "g" and "i" were used. Here it searches the entire string for uppercase or lowercase letters, noted in the output.

Checking for spaces

An example of fetching spaces with regex.

 console.log('   '.match(/\s/g))

Single characters 

const text = '1,2,3,4,5,6.a.b c!d?e'

const regex = /,/

console.log(text.split(regex))

In this example it will separate our string where there is a comma and ignoring it. This result is only possible using split().

output:

['1', '2', '3', '4', '5', '6.a.b c!d?e']

MetaCaracteres - Representatives

Metacharacters Name Meaning
. Point any one character (joker)
[] Set Allowed character set
[^] set denied Set of prohibited characters

MetaCharacters - Quantifiers

Metacharacters Name Meaning
? Optional zero or one
* Asterisk zero or more
+ Moist one or more
{n, m} Keys from n to m

MetaCharacters - Anchors

Metacharacters Name Meaning
^ Circumflex Inicio da linha
$ Cifrao Fim da linha
\b Borda Inicio ou fim da palavra

Other MetaCharacters

Metacharacters Name Meaning
\ Escape Using metacharacters with literals
\ or
() Group Defines a group
\1...\9 Rearview Rescue already defined groups

Using wildcard metacharacter:

const text = '1,2,3,4,,5,6,7,8,9,0'

console.logh(text.match(/1.2/g))
console.log(text.match(/1..2/g))

output

In this example above, it will search the entire string for a sequence that starts at "1" and ends at "2", but in the middle we have a wildcard, which means that it doesn't matter what character is there. In the second, it returns a null, because there are no two characters between 1 and 2.

Ways to search for space in string

In this example it will look for 3 spaces between "a" and "b". Below I made several ways that lead to the same result.

const text = 'a   b'

console.logh(text.match(/a   b/))
console.log(text.match(/a\s\s\sb/))
console.log(text.match(/a {3}b/))
console.log(text.match(/a\s{3}b/))
console.log(text.match(/\s+b/))

Let's see other ways to make a regex.

Gets all letters in an A-Z range.

console.log(text.match(/[a-z]/g))

You take all letters in a range of B-D.

console.log(text.match(/[b-d]/g))

Gets all numbers in a range from 2 to 4.

console.log(text.match(/[2-4]/g))

Gets all letters and numbers in the given range

console.log(text.match(/[A-Z1-3]/g))

Items.Title: 关于 Typescript 中的枚举 | All about Enum in Typescript
Items.Description:
枚举用于定义 Typescript 中的命名常量。 这是打字稿自己的功能，它不是 javascript 的类型级别扩展。 它是使用enum关键字定义的。

即，

  1. 数字枚举
  2. 字符串枚举
  3. 异构枚举

数字枚举。

 enum Numeric { First , Second , Third , Fourth } console . log ( Numeric . Second ); // 1
  1. 数字枚举的第一个成员未初始化，然后它从 0 开始， Second变为 1。
 enum Numeric { First = 1 , Second , Third , Fourth } console . log ( Numeric . Second ); // 2
  1. 数字枚举现在初始化为 1，因此从 1 开始， Second变为 2。

字符串枚举

用字符串文字初始化的枚举称为字符串枚举。

 enum StringEnum { A = " a " , B = " b " , C = " c " , D = " d " , }

异构枚举

数字和字符串枚举的混合。

 enum HeterogeneousEnum { No = 0 , Yes = ' YES ' }
  1. 枚举中不允许​​重复标识符。

使用枚举

一世。 访问任何成员作为枚举本身的属性。

 console . log ( Numeric . Second ); // 1

二. 使用枚举的名称声明类型。

 ( function myFunc ( word , number : Numeric ) { console . log ( word + ' ' + direction ); })( ' word ' , Numeric . Second ); // word 1
  1. 没有初始化器的枚举需要放在第一位。
 function getConstantValue () { return 1 ; } enum Numeric { first , second = getConstantValue (), } // first & second shouldn't be upside down.
  1. 没有初始化器的枚举应该在使用数字常量或其他枚举常量初始化的枚举之后。如下所示：
 enum Numeric { first = 2 , second = A , third }

但不像下面这样。

 enum Numeric { first = 2 , second = getConstantValue (), third }
  1. 每个枚举成员都有一个与之关联的值，该值可以是常量或计算值。
 enum constNComputeEnum { first = 1 , second = first , // reference to previously defined constant enum member. third = ( 1 + 2 ), // parenthesized constant enum expression, fourth = + true , // Unary operator applied. fifth = 1 & 2 // binary operator applied. }

  1. 在所有其他情况下，枚举成员被视为已计算。

  2. 具有字符串值成员的枚举中不允许​​计算值。 如下所示： 

 enum constNComputeEnum { first = 1 , second = ' second ' , third = constNComputeEnum . first , // not allowed }
  1. 将常量枚举表达式计算为 NaN 或 Infinity 是编译时错误。

Ideally, below is not accepted but somehow not throwing any error. 我检查了最新的打字稿，没有抛出任何错误。

 enum NaNNInfinity { first = 0 / 0 , // Result in NaN second = 1 / 0 , // Result in Infinity }

反向映射

数字枚举成员可以获得从枚举值到枚举名称的反向映射。

 enum reverseMappingEnum { first } const firstItem = reverseMappingEnum . first ; console . log ( reverseMappingEnum [ firstItem ]); // first

常量枚举。

为了避免生成额外的 js 代码，我们可以将任何枚举声明为 const。 这些在编译后被完全删除，并且不能有计算成员。 建议根本不要使用 const 枚举。

 const enum constEnum { first }

上面编译查看对应的js文件，什么也没有。

环境枚举

常规枚举：如果其前面的枚举成员被视为 const，则没有初始化程序的成员将被视为 const。
环境枚举：没有初始值设定项的成员将被视为已计算。

 enum AmbientEnum { first = 1 , second , third = 2 , }

对象与枚举

文字枚举

文字枚举成员是没有初始化值或具有初始化为的值的常量枚举成员

  • 任何字符串文字（例如“第一”，“第二”）
  • 任何数字文字（例如 1 到 100）
  • 应用于任何数字文字的一元减号（例如 -2、-4）

枚举成员也成为类型！

 enum Numeric { thirty , forty , } interface Person { age : Numeric . thirty ; id : number } let c : thirty = { age : Numeric . forty , id : 100 , };

将编译错误显示为： Type 'Numeric.forty' is not assignable to type 'Numeric.thirty'.

枚举类型本身有效地成为每个枚举成员的联合。

 enum Numeric { first , second , } function myFunc ( type : Numeric ) { if ( type !== Numeric . first || type !== Numeric . second ) { } }

将编译错误显示为： This condition will always return 'true' since the types 'Numeric.first' and 'Numeric.second' have no overlap.

原文:

Enums are used to define named constants in Typescript. It's the typescripts own feature which is not a type level extension of javascript. It is defined using enum keyword.

Typescript has three types of enums:

Namely,

  1. Numeric Enums
  2. String Enums
  3. Heterogeneous Enums

Numeric Enums. 

enum Numeric { First, Second, Third, Fourth
}
console.log(Numeric.Second); // 1
  1. A numeric enum's first member is not initialized then it starts from 0 and Second becomes 1. 
enum Numeric { First = 1, Second, Third, Fourth
}
console.log(Numeric.Second); // 2
  1. Numeric enum is now initialized with 1 hence starts from 1 and Second becomes 2.

String Enums

An enum which is initialized with string literal is called String enum.

enum StringEnum { A = "a", B = "b", C = "c", D = "d",
}

Heterogeneous enums

Mix of numeric and string enums.

enum HeterogeneousEnum { No = 0, Yes = 'YES'
}
  1. Duplicate Identifier is not allowed in Enum.

Using an enum

i. Access any member as the property of enum itself.

console.log(Numeric.Second); // 1

ii. Declare types using the name of the enum.

(function myFunc(word, number: Numeric) { console.log(word + ' ' + direction);
})('word', Numeric.Second);
// word 1
  1. Enum without initializers need to be first. 
function getConstantValue() { return 1;
} enum Numeric { first, second = getConstantValue(),
}
// first & second shouldn't be upside down.
  1. Enums without initializers should come after initialized enums with numeric constants or other enum constants.like below: 
enum Numeric { first = 2, second = A, third
}

But not like below.

enum Numeric { first = 2, second = getConstantValue(), third }
  1. Each enum member has a value associated with it which can be either constant or computed. 
enum constNComputeEnum { first = 1, second = first, // reference to previously defined constant enum member. third = (1 + 2), // parenthesized constant enum expression, fourth = +true, // Unary operator applied. fifth = 1 & 2 // binary operator applied.
}

  1. In All other cases enum member considered computed.

  2. Computed values are not permitted in an enum with string valued members. like below:

enum constNComputeEnum { first = 1, second = 'second', third = constNComputeEnum.first, // not allowed }
  1. It is a compile time error for constant enum expressions to be evaluated to NaN or Infinity.

Ideally, below is not accepted but somehow not throwing any error. I checked with latest typescript and is not throwing any error.

enum NaNNInfinity { first = 0 / 0, // Result in NaN second = 1 / 0, // Result in Infinity
}

Reverse Mapping

Numeric enum members can get a reverse mapping from enum values to enum names.

enum reverseMappingEnum { first
} const firstItem = reverseMappingEnum.first;
console.log(reverseMappingEnum[firstItem]); // first

const enums.

To avoid generating extra js code we can declare any enum as const. These are completely removed after compilation and can't have computed members. It is recommended to not use const enums at all.

const enum constEnum { first
}

Compile above and check its corresponding js file and you find nothing.

Ambient Enums

Regular Enums: Member that doesn't have initializer will be considered const if its preceding enum member considered const.
Ambient Enums: Member that doesn't have initializer will be considered as computed.

enum AmbientEnum { first = 1, second, third = 2,
}

Object vs Enums

Typescript Enum exactly equal to object with as const.

const enum Numeric { first, second, third, fourth, } const Numeric = { first: 0, second: 1, third: 2, fourth: 3, } as const;

Literal Enum

A literal enum member is a constant enum member with no initialized value, or with values that are initialized to

  • any string literal (e.g. "first", "second")
  • any numeric literal (e.g. 1 to 100)
  • a unary minus applied to any numeric literal (e.g. -2, -4)

Enum members also become types as well!

enum Numeric { thirty, forty, } interface Person { age: Numeric.thirty; id: number } let c: thirty = { age: Numeric.forty, id: 100, };

Shows compilation error as: Type 'Numeric.forty' is not assignable to type 'Numeric.thirty'.

Enum types themselves effectively become a union of each enum member.

 enum Numeric { first, second, } function myFunc(type: Numeric) { if (type !== Numeric.first || type !== Numeric.second) { } }

Shows compilation error as: This condition will always return 'true' since the types 'Numeric.first' and 'Numeric.second' have no overlap.
